what: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: OmopMultistate estimates individuals move clinically meaningful states time using cohort records OMOP Common Data Model. state represented cohort definition, transition matrix declares movements states possible. package provides workflow : prepare OMOP cohort_table long format used mstate; estimate state occupation probabilities overall specific initial states; repeat analysis across user-defined strata; return results standard summarised_result object visualise time.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: OmopMultistate part ecosystem packages defined omopgenerics. details ecosystem can read Tidy R programming OMOP Common Data Model book.

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: can install development version OmopMultistate GitHub :

title: What the Package Does (One Line, Title Case)
text: following example uses small mock OMOP CDM containing three states: treated, untreated, absorbing state death. First simplest possible case, analyse discontinuation acetaminophen. create 3 cohorts: acetaminophen, discontinuation acetaminophen (untreated) death. use CohortConstructor create cohorts. Now focus defining transitions, individuals can move : acetaminophen untreated acetaminophen death_cohort untreated acetaminophen untreated death_cohort records can occur date. resolve ties logical order, placing death last absorbing state. can prepare data long format used mstate fit models: interested summarising probabilities time can use summariseMultistateProbabilities function: run interactively, functions can print informational (ℹ) messages records excluded. example, person death record never enters eligible initial state contribute analysis. messages describe input data; R warnings. can visualise now results. States displayed order appear transition matrix:
code: library(OmopMultistate) library(omock) library(CohortConstructor) #> Warning: package 'CohortConstructor' was built under R version 4.4.3 library(CodelistGenerator) #> Warning: package 'CodelistGenerator' was built under R version 4.4.3 library(dplyr) #> Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.4.3  cdm <- mockCdmFromDataset(datasetName = "synpuf-1k", source = "duckdb")  cdm codes <- getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm = cdm, name = "acetaminophen", nameStyle = "{concept_name}")  # acetaminophen cohort cdm$acetaminophen <- conceptCohort(cdm = cdm, conceptSet = codes, name = "acetaminophen") #> ℹ Subsetting table drug_exposure using 6620 concepts with domain: drug. #> ℹ Combining tables. #> ℹ Creating cohort attributes. #> ℹ Applying cohort requirements. #> ℹ Merging overlapping records. #> ✔ Cohort acetaminophen created.  # death cohort cdm$death_cohort <- deathCohort(cdm = cdm, name = "death_cohort") #> ℹ Applying cohort requirements. #> ✔ Cohort death_cohort created.  # untreated cohort cdm$untreated <- cdm$acetaminophen |>   padCohortEnd(days = 1L, name = "untreated") |>   padCohortDate(days = 0L, indexDate = "cohort_end_date", cohortDate = "cohort_start_date") |>   renameCohort("untreated")  # bind them together cdm <- bind(cdm$acetaminophen, cdm$death_cohort, cdm$untreated, name = "my_study")  # the created cohort settings(cdm$my_study) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   cohort_definition_id cohort_name   cdm_version vocabulary_version #>                  <int> <chr>         <chr>       <chr>              #> 1                    1 acetaminophen 5.4         v5.0 29-APR-16     #> 2                    2 death_cohort  <NA>        <NA>               #> 3                    3 untreated     5.4         v5.0 29-APR-16     #> # ℹ 2 more variables: subset_cohort_table <chr>, subset_cohort_id <dbl> trans <- transMat(   x = list(c(2, 3), c(1, 3), c()),   names = c("acetaminophen", "untreated", "death_cohort") )  trans #>                to #> from            acetaminophen untreated death_cohort #>   acetaminophen            NA         1            2 #>   untreated                 3        NA            4 #>   death_cohort             NA        NA           NA stateHierarchy <- c("acetaminophen", "untreated", "death_cohort") msData <- prepareMultistateData(   cohort = cdm$my_study,   trans = trans,   stateHierarchy = stateHierarchy )  msData |>   arrange(subject_id, Tstart) |>   head(10) #> An object of class 'msdata' #>  #> Data: #>    subject_id from to trans Tstart Tstop status     from_name       to_name #> 1           1    1  2     1      0    30      1 acetaminophen     untreated #> 2           1    1  3     2      0    30      0 acetaminophen  death_cohort #> 3           1    2  1     3     30   373      1     untreated acetaminophen #> 4           1    2  3     4     30   373      0     untreated  death_cohort #> 5           1    1  2     1    373   403      1 acetaminophen     untreated #> 6           1    1  3     2    373   403      0 acetaminophen  death_cohort #> 7           1    2  1     3    403   532      1     untreated acetaminophen #> 8           1    2  3     4    403   532      0     untreated  death_cohort #> 9           1    1  2     1    532   552      1 acetaminophen     untreated #> 10          1    1  3     2    532   552      0 acetaminophen  death_cohort result <- summariseMultistateProbabilities(   cohort = cdm$my_study,   trans = trans,   stateHierarchy = stateHierarchy ) tidy(result) #> # A tibble: 9,552 × 9 #>    cdm_name  initial_state variable_name      variable_level probability #>    <chr>     <chr>         <chr>              <chr>                <dbl> #>  1 synpuf-1k overall       prob_acetaminophen 0                    1     #>  2 synpuf-1k overall       prob_untreated     0                    0     #>  3 synpuf-1k overall       prob_death_cohort  0                    0     #>  4 synpuf-1k overall       prob_acetaminophen 10                   0.770 #>  5 synpuf-1k overall       prob_untreated     10                   0.230 #>  6 synpuf-1k overall       prob_death_cohort  10                   0     #>  7 synpuf-1k overall       prob_acetaminophen 12                   0.772 #>  8 synpuf-1k overall       prob_untreated     12                   0.228 #>  9 synpuf-1k overall       prob_death_cohort  12                   0     #> 10 synpuf-1k overall       prob_acetaminophen 13                   0.774 #> # ℹ 9,542 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: cohort_table_name <chr>, follow_up_days <chr>, #> #   state_hierarchy <chr>, state_step <chr> result |>   filterGroup(initial_state == "acetaminophen") |>   plotMultistateProbabilities()

text: Prepare Multi-State Data OMOP Cohort Table

title: Prepare Multi-State Data from an OMOP Cohort Table — prepareMultistateData
code: prepareMultistateData(   cohort,   trans,   eventDate = "cohort_start_date",   censorDate = NULL,   stateHierarchy = character(),   stateStep = 0.01,   keepExtraColumns = TRUE )

title: Prepare Multi-State Data from an OMOP Cohort Table — prepareMultistateData
text: cohort cohort_table object containing one cohort state multi-state model. Cohort names must match state names trans. trans Transition matrix describing states transitions multi-state model. S number states multi-state model, trans S x S matrix, (,j)-element positive integer transition j possible multi-state model, NA otherwise. particular, diagonal elements NA. integers indicating possible transitions multi-state model sequentially numbered, 1,...,K, K number transitions. eventDate Name date column cohort identifies individual enters state. censorDate Name date column cohort identifies end follow-, inclusive. NULL, end individual's observation period used. stateHierarchy Character vector state names order used resolve states occur date, earlier entries occurring first. empty character vector applies hierarchy. stateStep Numeric increment used separate states occur date according stateHierarchy. keepExtraColumns Logical value indicating whether additional columns cohort retained prepared multi-state data.

title: Prepare Multi-State Data from an OMOP Cohort Table — prepareMultistateData
text: msdata object used mstate package.

title: Summarise Multi-State Occupation Probabilities over time — summariseMultistateProbabilities
text: cohort cohort_table object containing one cohort state multi-state model. Cohort names must match state names trans. trans Transition matrix describing states transitions multi-state model. S number states multi-state model, trans S x S matrix, (,j)-element positive integer transition j possible multi-state model, NA otherwise. particular, diagonal elements NA. integers indicating possible transitions multi-state model sequentially numbered, 1,...,K, K number transitions. strata List character vectors defining stratifications. character vector identifies one columns cohort; separate model fitted stratification. followUpDays Maximum number days follow-estimate state occupation probabilities. Use Inf include available follow-. eventDate Name date column cohort identifies individual enters state. censorDate Name date column cohort identifies end follow-, inclusive. NULL, end individual's observation period used. stateHierarchy Character vector state names order used resolve states occur date, earlier entries occurring first. empty character vector applies hierarchy. stateStep Numeric increment used separate states occur date according stateHierarchy.
